eventMetadata.context_description: Mark Hubbard holds a one-shot lead at 16-under after birdieing four of his last five holes in Round 3 for a 64, positioning him atop the ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic leaderboard entering the final round at Dunes Golf and Beach Club. Aaron Rai sits second at 15-under, with Kevin Roy third at 14-under, while Mac Meissner, Beau Hossler, and Brandt Snedeker share fourth at 13-under, locking in strong top-5 contention absent major collapses. Brooks Koepka surged to T7 at 11-under with a third-round 64 but trails by five; Christiaan Bezuidenhout and others lurk within six shots in this opposite-field stroke-play event. Early-round weather delays caused minor disruptions, but conditions stabilized at 72°F; multiple pre-tournament withdrawals like Marco Penge for health reasons thinned the 120-player field slightly, emphasizing current form and final-round tee times from the first tee.

markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Karmine Corp and GIANTX each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Karmine Corp vs GIANTX (BO3) - LEC Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Karmine Corp and GIANTX in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Karmine Corp" if Karmine Corp win the match against GIANTX.

This market will resolve to "GIANTX" if GIANTX win the match against Karmine Corp.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Karmine Corp leads LEC Spring 2026 regular season standings at 7-2, securing a top playoff seed after rebounding with a 2-1 win over Movistar KOI following a 0-2 upset loss to G2 Esports, while GIANTX clings to sixth place at 5-4 on the back of 0-2 sweeps by MKOI and G2 in Week 7. KC's commanding head-to-head history—7 series wins in 9 BO3s against GX, including a five-match streak with a recent 3-0 LEC Versus playoffs sweep—fuels trader consensus favoring their macro prowess from Yike's jungle paths and Caliste's ADC scaling. GIANTX boasts competitive laning but falters in teamfights, offering upset potential in this seeding-deciding matchup.

markets.question: Gabriel Strefezza: Anytime Goalscorer
markets.description: In the upcoming Serie A game between Parma Calcio 1913 and AS Roma, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Gabriel Strefezza is credited with a goal in the official box score within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". Own goals do not count. If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "50-50". If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not play, the market will resolve "No". The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Parma Calcio 1913 vs. AS Roma - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the Serie A game between Parma Calcio 1913 and AS Roma, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Roma sitting fifth in Serie A standings on 64 points after 35 matches, chasing a tight Champions League spot just behind Juventus, they face mid-table Parma (12th, 42 points) at Stadio Ennio Tardini on May 10 amid key absences impacting player props. Roma's Artem Dovbyk (thigh), Evan Ferguson (ankle), and captain Lorenzo Pellegrini (thigh) are out, shifting reliance to Donyell Malen (11 goals, 24 shots on target) and Paulo Dybala (1.3 SOT per match) for goals and shots overs, while Matias Soulé (5 assists) eyes creators markets. Parma's low-scoring form (0.71 goals per game, recent 0-2 loss to Inter Milan) favors Mateo Pellegrino props (8 goals), but their defense (1.20 conceded) and Roma's head-to-head dominance (14 wins to 3) bolster Giallorossi's attack despite injuries.

events.title: FC Košice vs. KFC Komárno - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Nike Liga game, scheduled for May 9 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Niké Liga relegation round, table-topping FC Košice face mid-table KFC Komárno at Stadion KFC Komárno on April 12, with Košice's stronger overall record (34 points from 26 games, 42 goals scored) and recent wins (W-W-D-D form) fueling trader optimism despite missing key midfielders Milan Dimun, Juraj Teplan, Matej Jakubek, and goalkeeper David Šipoš through June. Hosts Komárno (26 points, 28:37 goals) sit fourth in the group amid mixed results (L-D-W-D-D), without goalkeeper Benjamin Szaraz (calf), but boast a slight head-to-head edge (4 wins to Košice's 2, 5 draws) and low-scoring history (1.82 goals average). Home advantage and defensive resilience could test Košice's attack, though their momentum highlights upset potential in this survival scrap.

Lokomotiv's recent form shows inconsistency—losses to Krylya Sovetov (0-2) and Spartak Moscow (1-2), offset by a win over Orenburg (1-0)—while Baltika has drawn four straight before a 0-1 defeat to Akron Togliatti, boasting the league's stingiest defense (17 goals conceded). markets.question: Game 2: Both Teams Destroy Inhibitors?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. In League of Legends, each team has three inhibitors (one in each lane), located behind the inner turrets.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Karmine Corp and GIANTX each destroy at least one enemy inhibitor during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) do not destroy any enemy inhibitors.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams destroyed at least one inhibitor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Karmine Corp vs GIANTX (BO3) - LEC Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Karmine Corp and GIANTX in the LEC Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 10 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Karmine Corp" if Karmine Corp win the match against GIANTX.

This market will resolve to "GIANTX" if GIANTX win the match against Karmine Corp.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Karmine Corp leads LEC Spring 2026 regular season standings at 7-2, securing a top playoff seed after rebounding with a 2-1 win over Movistar KOI following a 0-2 upset loss to G2 Esports, while GIANTX clings to sixth place at 5-4 on the back of 0-2 sweeps by MKOI and G2 in Week 7. KC's commanding head-to-head history—7 series wins in 9 BO3s against GX, including a five-match streak with a recent 3-0 LEC Versus playoffs sweep—fuels trader consensus favoring their macro prowess from Yike's jungle paths and Caliste's ADC scaling. GIANTX boasts competitive laning but falters in teamfights, offering upset potential in this seeding-deciding matchup.

markets.question: Will US unemployment reach at least 5.0% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any seasonally adjusted unemployment rate (total unemployed, as a percent of the civilian labor force, official unemployment rate denoted as U-3) reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics in an “Employment Situation Report” for a reference month in 2026 is greater than or equal to the listed percentage. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve based on all previously published data up to that time. 

The resolution source for this market is the Monthly Employment Situation Report, published by the BLS every month at https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/empsit.htm, specifically the U-3 measure in Table A-15 for each month.

Note: the resolution source for this market reports unemployment to one decimal point. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

events.description:

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The relevant reports for this market are the Employment Situation Reports for January-December, 2026. This market may not resolve to “No” until the Employment Situation report for December 2026 is released. If no Employment Situation Report for December 2026 is released by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, however, this market will resolve at that time.

eventMetadata.context_description: The U.S. unemployment rate held steady at 4.3% in April 2026 per the Bureau of Labor Statistics' May 8 release, with nonfarm payrolls rising 115,000—surpassing consensus forecasts of around 60,000—signaling labor market resilience despite cooling job gains from prior months. FOMC's March projections peg the median rate at 4.4% by Q4 2026, reflecting expectations of modest softening amid persistent inflation above target and moderating consumer spending. Trader consensus on Polymarket prices in potential upside risks to 5% peaks from policy tightening or economic slowdowns, with key catalysts including the May jobs report due June 6 and the June 17-18 FOMC meeting, where updated dots could recalibrate rate path expectations.

markets.question: Will 50-74 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between April 27-May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from April 27, 2026, through May 3, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket delivers a 98.5% implied probability to 25-49 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz from April 27-May 3, 2026, anchored in satellite intelligence from Windward and Kpler logging just 5-19 daily vessels—down over 90% from pre-crisis norms of 138 crossings. The 2026 US-Iran standoff, featuring mutual blockades, vessel seizures, and sporadic attacks since March, has throttled commercial flows to selective Iran-linked tankers, with preliminary IMF Portwatch data tallying around 33 total transit calls. This skin-in-the-game positioning reflects sustained risk aversion amid elevated insurance premiums and oil supply disruptions. Challenges could arise from final Portwatch revisions capturing underreported dark fleet activity or a last-minute de-escalation boosting counts above 50.

markets.question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump awaits Iran's response to a recent US peace proposal demanding verifiable curbs on nuclear development, sanctions relief, and an end to proxy conflicts, amid a fragile ceasefire holding since April following failed Pakistani-mediated talks in Islamabad. The 2026 war, sparked by US-Israeli strikes on February 28 after Iran's protest crackdown, has seen competing blockades of the Strait of Hormuz disrupt global trade, with fresh flare-ups reported this week. No permanent deal has emerged despite indirect diplomacy in Geneva and Oman, as Tehran deems US terms maximalist; upcoming responses or summits could shift negotiations, though historical impasses persist.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 2, or if Map 2 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 2 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Imperial Academy vs Infinite (BO3) - United21 Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 1 match between Imperial Academy and Infinite in the United21 Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 9 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Imperial Academy" if Imperial Academy win the match against Infinite.

This market will resolve to "Infinite" if Infinite win the match against Imperial Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Imperial Academy advanced to the United21 Season 47 playoffs upper bracket quarterfinal as undefeated Group A winners, securing a 2-0 record with dominant 2-1 and 2-0 victories over LPH Gaming and Sangal ALTERS, highlighted by strong performances on Inferno and Dust II amid a stable young roster averaging 18.9 years old. Infinite earned Group D runner-up via a 2-1 group stage (beating AaB esport twice 2-1 but losing 0-2 to Misa Esports), bolstered by recent tournament triumphs like winning United21 Season 46 and Parken Challenger Championship #5, with a higher HLTV ranking (#59) and nine-match win streak entering the BO3 clash on April 9. Stand-in fnl for Blytz on Infinite's map 1 added minor uncertainty, while both squads' map pool strengths on Ancient, Mirage, and Nuke promised a competitive matchup driven by group momentum and form.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

Donald Trump's 6.5% trails closely on speculation of U.S.-brokered deals echoing Abraham Accords momentum, while Volodymyr Zelenskyy's 5.8% reflects his formal nomination for Ukrainian resilience against invasion. UNRWA (4.2%) and Pope Leo XIV (3.8%) gain from humanitarian crises and the new pontiff's Easter peace appeals, but the fragmented field underscores intense competition—differentiated by mediation records (Qatar's Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani), judicial activism (ICJ), and global advocacy. markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the de facto leader of Venezuela at the end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of Venezuela on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary executive authority over the Venezuelan state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Venezuela’s territory at the specified time.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.

markets.question: Will the Fed decrease interest rates by 50+ bps after the September 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal funds range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's September 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for September 15-16, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their September meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects caution around the Federal Reserve's September 15-16, 2026, FOMC meeting, where the target range remains at 3.50%-3.75% following the April 29 hold amid rising inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year, up from 2.4%, while April nonfarm payrolls added 115,000 jobs and unemployment held steady at 4.3%, signaling labor market resilience that tempers rate-cut expectations. The March dot plot median projects a 3.4% fed funds rate by year-end 2026, implying modest easing ahead, but sticky core inflation and balanced risks noted in recent statements suggest no immediate action. Key catalysts include tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12 and the June 16-17 meeting, which could recalibrate market-implied paths via fed funds futures.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 215-239 tweets from May 11 to May 13, 2026?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Letitia James be arrested before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual is arrested by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being briefly detained but not arrested (e.g. such as in the South Korean judicial system when an individual is awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant)
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: Haunted House vs THUNDER dOWNUNDER (BO3) - Asian Champions League Group B
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Haunted House and THUNDER dOWNUNDER in the Asian Champions League Group B, initially scheduled for May 11 at 3:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Haunted House" if Haunted House win the match against THUNDER dOWNUNDER.

This market will resolve to "THUNDER dOWNUNDER" if THUNDER dOWNUNDER win the match against Haunted House.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for tech IPOs before 2027 hinges on recent confidential S-1 filings reported for SpaceX—targeting a $2 trillion valuation in June-July 2026—and Discord, alongside preparations by Databricks amid 65% revenue growth to $4 billion. Stripe's $159 billion liquidity deal signals readiness despite no formal filing, while OpenAI and Anthropic advance legal groundwork for second-half listings, fueled by AI infrastructure demand. Improving market conditions post-2025 rebound, with Cerebras Systems eyeing a May debut, bolster consensus on a 2026 wave. Key catalysts include S-1 public disclosures, roadshows, and earnings beats; delays from regulatory scrutiny or volatility remain risks.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy, Team Spirit's academy squad with a February 2026 roster refresh featuring Kiryasoo, VILBy, and s1nside, entered Exort Series #25 Main Stage Round 1 BO3 against Wave Esports as the implied favorite based on organizational backing and prior head-to-head edges in Exort events. Wave, HLTV #251 with March roster additions of Katalic, sheezy, and Belof, showed competitive qualifier form including 2-0 wins over BIG Academy and LEO but struggled with consistency on Ancient and Dust2. Traders weighed roster cohesion in the Swiss format—where 3-0 advances to playoffs—pistol rounds, and map pools amid a stable CS2 meta, with no major patch shifts or withdrawals reported pre-match.

markets.question: Will Jordan win Group J in the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins Group J in the 2026 FIFA World Cup group stage, scheduled for June 11-27, 2026.

If multiple teams tie as group winners, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If the World Cup group stage is cancelled, postponed after September 30, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this group within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champions Argentina lead trader consensus at 76.5% implied probability to top Group J, bolstered by coach Lionel Scaloni's May 5 confirmation of their final 26-man pre-tournament roster featuring fit captain Lionel Messi, Julián Álvarez, and Enzo Fernández, with no major injury concerns amid intense camp drills emphasizing mentality after their 2022 Saudi Arabia upset reminder. Austria's 16.5% reflects Ralf Rangnick's high-pressing 4-2-3-1 system and recent UEFA playoff resilience against Turkey, positioning them as the prime challenger via midfield transitions led by Marcel Sabitzer and David Alaba. Algeria's 8.2% stems from Riyad Mahrez's counterattacking threat and CAF qualifier grit over Egypt and Senegal, while Jordan's debutant 1.6% underscores their underdog compact block despite Musa Al-Taamari's pace, with camps firing but facing steep talent gaps just weeks from kickoff.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rep. Mike Collins holds a commanding lead in the Georgia Republican U.S. Senate primary per the latest Quantus Insights poll from early May, capturing 33% support against Derek Dooley's 23% and Rep. Buddy Carter's 14% among likely voters, with 28% undecided as the May 19 contest nears and early voting continues. Collins' edge stems from superior Q1 fundraising—outpacing rivals combined—strong debate performances, including a recent April 26 forum, and endorsements like the Senate Conservatives Fund, appealing to the Trump-aligned GOP base despite Gov. Brian Kemp's backing of Dooley. Traders' 83% implied probability on Collins reflects consensus on his momentum to secure a majority and avoid a runoff, while Dooley's recent surge accounts for his 15% share and Carter's fade explains his slim odds.

markets.question: Will Seattle have between 0.5 and 1 inches of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Seattle between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for May 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Seattle City Area" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=sew link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

If the reported data falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Lagos be 30°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Murtala Muhammad International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ng/lagos/DNMM.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts slightly toward a 33°C high in Lagos on May 12, mirroring the Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet)'s recent short-term forecasts indicating afternoon peaks around 33°C amid the rainy season onset, with 34°C a close contender due to model spread in the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and Global Forecast System (GFS). High cloudiness, peaking at 88% historically on this date, and strengthening sea breezes from the Gulf of Guinea introduce uncertainty, potentially capping temperatures 1–2°C below clearer days, while urban heat island effects in Lagos push toward the upper end of the 32–34°C climatological range for early May. NiMet's 2026 seasonal outlook flags warmer-than-average conditions overall. Watch for NiMet's May 11 daily bulletin and May 10 observations, which could sharpen the closely matched 30.5% and 28% implied probabilities.

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns Democrats a 60.5% implied probability of winning the 2028 US presidential election over Republicans at 38.5%, driven by anticipation of Democratic gains in the 2026 midterms—where markets favor Democrats retaking the House (87%) and Senate (55%). This reflects the historical midterm penalty for the president's party, currently Republicans under President Trump, potentially eroding GOP momentum ahead of the open-seat race with no incumbent advantage. Odds surged to an all-time high near 61% in mid-April amid early national polling averages showing Democrats leading hypothetical matchups and high trading volume on Democratic nominee markets favoring figures like Gavin Newsom. Upcoming 2026 congressional elections remain the pivotal catalyst, with battleground states and turnout dynamics key to shifting probabilities.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Jannik Sinner at 70.5% implied probability to win the 2026 Men's French Open, driven by his dominant clay-court swing capped by straight-sets defeats of Carlos Alcaraz in the Monte-Carlo Masters final and Alexander Zverev in Madrid last weekend, marking five straight Masters 1000 titles and a 30-2 season record. Alcaraz's right wrist injury—forcing his April 24 withdrawal from Roland Garros—clears the defending champion's path, while Novak Djokovic enters with zero clay wins in 2026 after minimal prep and an early Rome exit. Zverev (6.6%) gains from consistent deep runs and prior Roland Garros final experience, with young risers like Rafael Jodar (3.4%) and Arthur Fils impressing via Madrid quarters and Barcelona title, respectively, amid a wide-open field post-Alcaraz.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club enters final-round play with Alex Fitzpatrick and Kristoffer Reitan co-leading at 13-under through two holes, Sungjae Im one back at 12-under, and a tight cluster—including Cameron Young (-11), Rickie Fowler, Tommy Fleetwood, and Nicolai Højgaard (all -10)—battling for top-10 positions within four strokes overall. Pre-event withdrawals by Scottie Scheffler (resting for PGA Championship), Collin Morikawa (back injury), and Jake Knapp (thumb) depleted the signature-event field, boosting contention for others amid low scores like Young's third-round 63. Rory McIlroy sits T19 at 5-under despite strong course history, as ball-striking and putting on the long par-71 layout remain pivotal for volatile top-10 outcomes.

markets.question: Israel x Hezbollah diplomatic meeting by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of Israel and Hezbollah who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding Israel-Hezbollah relations on behalf of their governments/leadership structures. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the government of Israel and Hezbollah leadership, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-mediated direct talks between Israel and Lebanon, involving their ambassadors in Washington, marked the first such diplomatic engagement in decades on April 14, with follow-up sessions amid a fragile ceasefire following escalated Israel-Hezbollah clashes. Hezbollah has rejected participation, firing rockets during initial meetings and signaling openness only to indirect negotiations via intermediaries, while opposing disarmament demands central to Israeli positions. No direct Israel-Hezbollah diplomatic contact has occurred, as the group views state-level talks as a threat. Traders watch upcoming May 14-15 Washington negotiations for potential de-escalation signals, though ongoing strikes and Hezbollah threats sustain uncertainty over any bilateral meeting by the market's resolution.

markets.question: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Saudi Professional League game between Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club, scheduled for April 10 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Al Shabab Saudi Club and Al Ittihad Saudi Club each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on slstat.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ittihad sit sixth in the Saudi Pro League table with 42 points from 26 matches, but their recent form has faltered with just one win in the last five games (1W-2D-2L), exacerbated by the winter departures of stars Karim Benzema to Al Hilal and N'Golo Kanté, leaving gaps in attack and midfield amid fan frustration ahead of a King's Cup semi-final. Al Shabab, 12th on 29 points, host at SHG Arena on April 10 showing sharper scoring edge lately (three wins in five, 12 goals), buoyed by home strength despite mid-table position and forward Mohammed Harbush sidelined long-term with a cruciate injury. Al Ittihad hold a strong head-to-head edge, unbeaten in the last five meetings, setting up a competitive Riyadh clash influenced by rest advantages and tactical adjustments.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mark Hubbard holds a one-shot lead at 16-under after birdieing four of his last five holes in Round 3 for a 64, positioning him atop the ONEflight Myrtle Beach Classic leaderboard entering the final round at Dunes Golf and Beach Club. Aaron Rai sits second at 15-under, with Kevin Roy third at 14-under, while Mac Meissner, Beau Hossler, and Brandt Snedeker share fourth at 13-under, locking in strong top-5 contention absent major collapses. Brooks Koepka surged to T7 at 11-under with a third-round 64 but trails by five; Christiaan Bezuidenhout and others lurk within six shots in this opposite-field stroke-play event. markets.question: Will Stephanie Luciano win by KO or TKO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Stephanie Luciano defeats Carol Foro at UFC Fight Night: Adesanya vs. Pyfer, scheduled for March 28, 2026, by KO or TKO, including referee stoppage, doctor stoppage, or corner stoppage. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If the fight ends in a draw or by disqualification (either fighter), it will resolve "No."

If the bout is ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond April 11, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

events.title: UFC Fight Night: Stephanie Luciano vs. Carol Foro (Women's Strawweight, Prelims)
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Stephanie Luciano" if Stephanie Luciano is officially declared the winner of the fight against Carol Foro at UFC Fight Night: Adesanya vs. Pyfer, scheduled for March 28, 2026.

It will resolve to "Carol Foro" if Carol Foro is officially declared the winner.

If the fight is declared a draw or technical draw, ruled a No Contest, not scored, canceled, or postponed beyond April 11, 2026, this market will resolve "50-50."

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

markets.question: Spread: US Catanzaro 1929 (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Serie B game, scheduled for May 12 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "US Catanzaro 1929" if US Catanzaro 1929 win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "US Avellino 1912".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaserieb.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: US Catanzaro 1929 vs. US Avellino 1912 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Serie B game, scheduled for May 12 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Catanzaro hosts Avellino in the Serie B promotion playoff preliminary round at Stadio Nicola Ceravolo, with the 5th-placed Giallorossi holding a superior regular-season record of 59 points from 38 matches compared to Avellino's mid-table finish around 8th. Their most recent encounter on April 11 ended 1-1 at Avellino, following Catanzaro's 1-0 home win in December 2025, giving the hosts an unbeaten streak in recent head-to-heads. Both sides enter off losses—Catanzaro 3-1 to Cesena and Avellino 1-0 at Empoli—highlighting defensive vulnerabilities amid end-of-season fatigue. Key absences include Catanzaro's Alphadjo Cissè (adductor injury), while home advantage and higher goal tally (62 scored) position Catanzaro as trader consensus favorite in this high-stakes knockout clash.

markets.question: Will David Njoku play for Indianapolis Colts in 2026-27?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next team David Njoku officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If David Njoku does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If David Njoku joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku remains unsigned deep into the 2026 NFL offseason following his February announcement parting ways with the Browns after nine seasons, hampered by knee injuries that sidelined him for 11 games across 2024-25 despite prior Pro Bowl production. Post-draft, trader consensus reflects a fragmented market with no frontrunner, as cap-flexible, TE-needy contenders like the Bengals (weapons for Burrow), Commanders (building around Daniels), and Chargers (recent visit per Rapoport) cluster at 47-49% implied probabilities. Lingering interest from Ravens (prior visit), Dolphins (April sighting), and potential Falcons scenarios via Pitts trade keeps the race tight, underscoring Njoku's value as an affordable veteran amid stalled free agency dynamics.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick (R) holds a commanding fundraising lead with over $7.6 million cash on hand entering the May 19 Democratic primary, where Bucks County Commissioner Bob Harvie leads Lucia Simonelli as the likely challenger in this D+1 battleground district pivotal to Democrats' House majority flip path amid Republicans' slim 218-214 edge. Trader consensus tilts slightly Democratic at 52.5% despite a March poll showing Fitzpatrick ahead 48-42 over Harvie, reflecting Fitzpatrick's recent bipartisan votes—including support for Haitian TPS extension on April 16 and Canada tariff repeal—that have drawn GOP primary ire and depressed base enthusiasm. Separation could arise from the primary outcome, national midterm headwinds, or heavy outside spending targeting Bucks County's swing voters.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls consistently position incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva ahead in first-round scenarios for Brazil's October 4, 2026, presidential election, with Flávio Bolsonaro emerging as the clear runner-up at 34-40%, driving trader consensus to price him at 66.5% for second place. Surveys from early May, including Ideia (May 1-5: Lula 40%, Flávio 36%) and Real Time Big Data (May 2-4: Lula 40%, Flávio 34%), alongside late April AtlasIntel (Lula 47%, Flávio 40%), show Flávio consolidating the right-wing vote amid fragmentation among governors like Ronaldo Caiado (3-6%), Romeu Zema (3-4%), and outsider Renan Santos (1-5%). Lula's 19.5% implied probability for second reflects his polling lead, though tightening margins signal a competitive race; no major events in the past week, but party conventions loom as potential catalysts.

Recent first-rehearsal clips from Vienna, released in the last 48 hours, showcase polished vocal deliveries and jury-pleasing stagings—Goodrem's powerhouse ballad, Monroe's classical drama, Lampenius' violin flair bolstered by their May 1 Eurojury victory, and Lund's emotive storytelling—fueling the deadlock. Juries historically favor strong compositions over spectacle, but secret votes and semi-final performances next week could swing momentum amid fierce Nordic and Down Under competition.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs REKONIX (BO3) - 1win Essence Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy's flawless 4-0 group stage record and 8-0 map differential in 1win Essence I Group B, capped by a 2-0 sweep over REKONIX on May 2, underpin trader consensus favoring them heavily in this BO3 matchup. The European veterans—mid No!ob, offlaner Davai Lama, supports OmaR and GH under coach KuroKy—leverage superior macro execution and draft flexibility on patch 7.41c against REKONIX's rising Indonesian roster of carry Jikroy, mid inYourdreaM, offlaner Fbz, and supports dalul/Varizh, whose 2-2 group showing reflects regional strength but international growing pains. REKONIX's 64% seasonal winrate hints at upset potential via aggressive laning, though NGX's experience tilts probabilities amid group survival stakes.

markets.question: US obtains Iranian enriched uranium by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government or military officially announces or confirms that it has gained possession of any quantity of enriched uranium previously controlled by Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

“Possession” means that the United States has actual physical custody or control of the enriched uranium, whether held within U.S. territory or elsewhere. Announcements of deals, agreements, commitments, or plans under which the United States would acquire possession of Iranian enriched uranium at a later time will not qualify.

Qualifying possession of Iranian enriched uranium may be acquired through any means, including through an agreed surrender or seizure.

A widespread consensus of credible reporting that the United States has gained possession of Iranian enriched uranium will also qualify for a “Yes” resolution, even if the United States makes no formal announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a widespread consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing US-Iran negotiations, intensified after recent hostilities, center on Tehran surrendering its estimated 440kg stockpile of 60% enriched uranium—near weapons-grade levels per IAEA assessments—in exchange for sanctions relief and a ceasefire framework. President Trump stated on May 10 that Washington has the material surveilled and will prevent Iran from weaponizing it, while reports indicate talks floated shipping the uranium to the US, though Iranian officials deem it "sacred" and non-transferable. On May 8, the US seized 13.5kg of enriched uranium from Venezuela amid frustrations over Iran, but this does not qualify for market resolution, which requires official US confirmation of Iranian material possession. Traders weigh diplomatic progress against Tehran's resistance and potential escalation, with no deal finalized as of May 10; upcoming talks could clarify timelines.

markets.question: Will Partido dos Trabalhadores (PT) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors the Partido Liberal (PL) to secure the most seats in Brazil's October 4, 2026, Senate election, where 54 seats across states and the Distrito Federal will renew two-thirds of the chamber, with top vote-getters per state claiming victory. Recent Genial/Quaest polls from late April 2026, published in early May, show PL candidates like Cláudio Castro leading in Rio de Janeiro and Filipe Barros competitive in Paraná, alongside strong showings in Minas Gerais and Pernambuco, positioning PL for multiple wins amid opposition momentum from the Senate's rejection of President Lula's Supreme Court nominee Jorge Messias. PSB's Simone Tebet leads in São Paulo, but PL's broader viability in battleground states and potential coattails from Flávio Bolsonaro's tied presidential polls underpin the lopsided odds, though shifting voter turnout or scandals could alter outcomes before candidate registrations close.

markets.question: Will D.C. United win the 2026 MLS Cup?

markets.question: Will Pope Leo XIV publish his encyclical by May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Pope Leo XIV publishes his encyclical by May 15, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For a letter to qualify it must be commonly understood as an encyclical or declared as an encyclical upon release. Other forms of papal brief, such as a papal bull, will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be information from Pope Leo XIV, official information from the Catholic Church, or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tilts toward "No" at 62% implied probability for Pope Leo XIV publishing his first encyclical by May 15, driven by the absence of any official Vatican announcement or confirmed release date despite earlier rumors. Reports from mid-March hinted at a post-Easter rollout for the anticipated social encyclical, with recent Africa trip remarks four days ago previewing themes like Church social doctrine, but no draft or publication timeline has materialized nearly a year into his May 2025 papacy. His prior "Dilexi te" apostolic exhortation in October 2025 addressed poverty but was not a full encyclical, underscoring preparation delays typical for such weighty documents. With just 13 days left, traders weigh the tight window against historical papal pacing, though a surprise announcement remains possible amid his active public engagements.

events.title: 1. FC Union Berlin vs. FC Augsburg
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between 1. FC Union Berlin and FC Augsburg.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices all outcomes at even money, capturing the razor-thin margins in this Bundesliga clash at An der alten Försterei, where Union Berlin's staunch home record tempers FC Augsburg's hot streak. Union sit 13th after a winless run in six matches—three draws, three losses, including a gritty 2-2 versus Köln and 1-1 with St. Pauli—but they've netted consistently without clean sheets. Augsburg, 9th in the table, boast five unbeaten games (three wins, two draws), scoring nine goals yet failing to keep a clean sheet in eight, vulnerable away. Mutual injury woes, like Union's absent goalkeeper Frederik Rønnow (thigh) and Robert Skov (calf), plus Augsburg's Mads Pedersen sidelined, alongside a balanced head-to-head history, keep probabilities tightly contested heading into matchday 34.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 22, 2026, parliamentary elections in Slovenia, which produced a hung National Assembly with no outright majority, incumbent Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement conceded failure to form a coalition on April 20 after exhaustive talks. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to name a prime minister-designate on April 25, shifting initiative to parliamentary parties. Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS), the runner-up, has since led center-right coalition negotiations with New Slovenia (NSi), Modern Centre Party (SMC), and others, advancing a slim majority through ministry trims and signed agreements as of early May. This momentum reflects trader consensus at 92% for Janša, bolstered by historical patterns of right-bloc unity. Challenges could arise from negotiation breakdowns, key partner defections like NSi, or a no-confidence pivot triggering snap elections.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Darth Vader" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The specified metric will be considered as reported in the company’s official earnings materials. Subsequent revisions will not be considered.

If the specified company’s official earnings materials for the specified quarter are released, and the specified metric is not included, this market will resolve to “No”. 
 
If the specified company does not release quarterly earnings materials for the specified quarter by July 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.
