eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon's share price has climbed to $272.68 as of the May 8, 2026 close, up over 27% in April, propelled by Q1 earnings that beat estimates with $181.5 billion in revenue (+17% year-over-year) and EPS of $2.78 versus $1.63 expected. AWS growth accelerated 28% to $37.6 billion—the fastest in 15 quarters—fueled by AI demand from partnerships like Anthropic, alongside record 13.1% operating margins reflecting e-commerce efficiency. A $200 billion capex plan for AI infrastructure sparked short-term caution, but analyst consensus targets $310-$370 imply 14-36% upside, with traders monitoring May retail sales data and FOMC signals for month-end pricing amid elevated volatility.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Europe at 71.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by UEFA's 16 qualification slots filled with top-ranked powerhouses like Spain (seeded 1st), France (3rd), England (4th), and Portugal (6th), bolstered by recent Euro triumphs and superior depth in the expanded 48-team field. The March 2026 qualifier playoffs concluded with upsets like Italy's elimination, redistributing strength among other Europeans, while the group draw yielded favorable paths avoiding early clashes among elites. South America holds 20.5% on defending champions Argentina (2nd seed) and Brazil (5th), despite CONMEBOL securing six direct spots. Africa (3.5%), Asia (3.4%), North America (2.5% including hosts USA, Mexico, Canada), and Oceania (0.3%) trail due to historical knockout-stage struggles and fewer top-10 squads.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Australia (Delta Goodrem's "Eclipse") and France (Monroe's operatic "Regarde!") as virtual co-frontrunners for the Eurovision 2026 jury winner at 25.5% and 24% implied probabilities, respectively, with Finland (Linda Lampenius & Pete Parkkonen's "Liekinheitin") and Denmark (Søren Torpegaard Lund's "Før Vi Går Hjem") close behind at 19% each in this razor-thin contest. Recent first-rehearsal clips from Vienna, released in the last 48 hours, showcase polished vocal deliveries and jury-pleasing stagings—Goodrem's powerhouse ballad, Monroe's classical drama, Lampenius' violin flair bolstered by their May 1 Eurojury victory, and Lund's emotive storytelling—fueling the deadlock. Juries historically favor strong compositions over spectacle, but secret votes and semi-final performances next week could swing momentum amid fierce Nordic and Down Under competition.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $2.00 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=NGD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

Trader consensus reflects the EIA's latest storage report, which revealed a 63 Bcf injection for the week ended May 1—below the 72 Bcf forecast—driven by a recent monthly production drop to around 110 Bcf/d and surging LNG exports near record levels, propelling futures to three-week highs. Inventories at 2,205 Bcf, 20% above the five-year average, temper gains during the shoulder season. Key catalysts include Thursday's EIA storage update and end-May weather outlooks for power demand.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Pentagon's announcement on May 2 of withdrawing 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months—amid disputes with NATO allies over base access during the Iran conflict—has intensified transatlantic frictions and revived President Trump's criticisms of uneven burden-sharing. This partial drawdown signals leverage tactics rather than full exit, as Article 13 withdrawal requires one-year notice but is constrained by Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act, mandating congressional approval or a two-thirds Senate vote amid bipartisan resistance from senators like Roger Wicker and Chuck Schumer. Traders price low odds on withdrawal, prioritizing legal barriers and institutional pressures over rhetoric, with no imminent votes or summits altering the status quo.

The March trailer drop fueled buzz for its high-stakes heist premise and Ritchie's signature stylish action, though his recent films like "Wrath of Man" (67%) and "The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare" (71%) show mixed critical reception for formulaic pacing versus kinetic energy. Key watch: embargo lift likely post-premiere, with scores solidifying by May 18 amid box office tracking and early audience reactions.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the TX-38 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the TX-38 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Spread: AD Municipal Liberia (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Liga Promerica game, scheduled for May 10 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AD Municipal Liberia" if AD Municipal Liberia win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Deportivo Saprissa".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on unafut.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Deportivo Saprissa, second in the Primera División Clausura standings with 31 points from 17 matches, host AD Municipal Liberia, who sit third on 30 points after a stunning 2-1 upset victory at Estadio Ricardo Saprissa Aymá on April 12—Liberia's first win there in 23 head-to-head meetings, ending Saprissa's unbeaten streak despite their prior 5-1 cup rout on March 29. Saprissa's recent form shows prolific scoring (10 goals in last five), but defensive lapses allowed late concessions, boosting Liberia's confidence amid the tight title race. No major injuries reported from official team updates; home advantage and historical dominance (19 wins, 7 draws, 2 losses overall) favor Saprissa rebound, though Liberia's away resilience adds upset potential for props like total goals (averaging 3.21 H2H).

markets.question: Will François Hollande win the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

Édouard Philippe trails closely at 19.5% as the strongest second-round challenger, projected to edge Bardella 51-52% in runoffs amid Horizons' momentum post his March municipal reelection. The race stays tight due to a fragmented field with over 30 declared hopefuls, left-wing splits after Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 3 candidacy announcement, and no unified anti-far-right front. Separation could arise from Le Pen's appeal verdict, party primaries, or key endorsements ahead of the April 2027 vote.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Istanbul be 19°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Istanbul Airport in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LTFM

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Paris be 17°C on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Paris-Le Bourget Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/fr/bonneuil-en-france/LFPB.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This below-normal pattern—versus the May climatological average of 18°C—stems from cool air advection at 850 hPa levels and reduced insolation from variable cloud cover, as shown in recent ECMWF and GFS ensemble runs with a 2-3°C spread. Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 16°C (35.5%) due to potential afternoon clearing boosting peak hourly temperatures at official stations like Paris-Montsouris, though persistent overcast could cap at 15°C; watch 12z model updates today for refinements.

markets.question: Will Delcy Rodríguez be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

markets.question: Will the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting be on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed date (Pakistan Standard Time) on which the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran occurs.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If no qualifying meeting occurs by May 10, 2026, 11:59 PM Pakistan Standard Time, this market will resolve to “No Meeting before May 11”.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors no US-Iran diplomatic meeting before May 11 at 99.6%, reflecting the absence of any announced in-person talks amid fragile ceasefire holding in the Strait of Hormuz and stalled indirect negotiations. As of May 10, the US awaits Iran's response to its latest peace proposal—a potential one-page memorandum to end hostilities—while envoy Witkoff noted ongoing conversations but President Trump deemed Tehran's counteroffer unacceptable, with no venue or date publicized. Earlier rounds in Oman and Geneva concluded without breakthroughs, prioritizing nuclear curbs and sanctions relief. This commanding position could shift via sudden escalation, such as tanker incidents prompting emergency mediation, or rapid agreement announcement leading to impromptu Vienna or Muscat summit.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Six Seven" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 11, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 17, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

This flagship event anchors trader focus for the week of May 17-23, as no other public speeches or appearances are confirmed in recent White House schedules. Recent developments include routine Oval Office policy meetings, Rose Garden addresses like the May 8 remarks, and proclamations on fitness and military spouses, signaling continued emphasis on domestic priorities. Upcoming schedule releases or pre-event briefings could refine expectations on phrasing around national renewal and executive actions.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 33°C or higher on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Central Weather Administration (CWA) observations from Taipei Songshan Airport Station—the market's resolution source—confirm today's highest temperature peaked at 29°C amid persistent cloud cover and scattered showers that capped daytime heating. This aligns with seasonal norms for early May in Taipei, where average highs hover around 28–29°C under the transitioning subtropical monsoon influence, suppressing rapid intensification. Trader consensus at 100% implied probability for 29°C reflects this verified observational data, with negligible capital on higher bins due to no late-afternoon spikes in real-time feeds. Realistic challenges include rare post-preliminary data revisions from quality control or unrepresentative station microclimate effects, though CWA final tallies rarely shift beyond 0.5°C; watch evening updates for closure.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

While low probabilities on locations like China or Russia reflect faint speculation on neutral-site diplomacy, shifts would require sudden breakthroughs such as Ukraine peace progress, escalation de-escalation signals, or third-party mediation prompting an urgent venue like Turkey or Switzerland.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures for June delivery (GC) trade near $4,740 per ounce, reflecting trader consensus on sustained upside driven by record central bank purchases—244 tonnes net in Q1 2026, on track for 850 tonnes annually amid geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. A weakening US Dollar Index around 97.8 bolsters appeal, while the Federal Reserve's federal funds rate holds at 3.5-3.75%, anchoring low real yields that favor non-yielding assets. Recent climbs above $4,720 mark the highest since late April, fueled by weekly gains over 2%. Key catalysts ahead include May CPI data and the June 17-18 FOMC meeting, where rate-cut odds languish below 4%, potentially pressuring prices if inflation persists. Polymarket odds aggregate this skin-in-the-game sentiment, pricing probabilities around pivotal thresholds like $5,000.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Spread: Rangers FC (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Scottish Premiership game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Rangers FC" if Rangers FC win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Hibernian FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on spfl.co.uk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Rangers FC vs. Hibernian FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Scottish Premiership game, scheduled for May 13 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rangers host Hibernian at Ibrox in the Scottish Premiership Championship Round on May 13, amid a fiercely contested title race where Hearts lead with 70 points from 33 games, Rangers sit second on 69, and Celtic trail on 67. Rangers' trader consensus reflects urgency after a shocking 2-3 home loss to Motherwell on April 26—snapping momentum from their 6-3 thrashing of Falkirk—while Hibs, fifth in the table, fell 1-2 to Hearts that weekend. Rangers boast an unbeaten run in recent head-to-heads (three wins, one draw since October), strong Ibrox form, but face injury concerns with Ryan Naderi sidelined long-term and Tuur Rommens doubtful; Hibs monitor Josh Mulligan's ankle recovery. Late-season stakes amplify pressure for the hosts.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Energy prices surged 12.5% over the prior year, fueling nowcasts like Cleveland Fed's projecting annualized headline CPI near 8.5% for May and core PCE at 3.7%. The New York Fed noted rising short-term inflation expectations on May 7, amid 5-year, 5-year forward breakevens steady near 2.3%. Upside risks persist from tariffs and robust labor markets; key catalysts include April CPI on May 12 and June 16-17 FOMC, where policy may pivot toward hikes if data sticks above 3%.

markets.question: Will Google have the second highest estimated revenue for May 11–May 17, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the model family that has the second-highest total estimated inference revenue based on Anera's data published at https://anera.markets/intelligence/model-family for the specified week.

The week’s values will be determined by summing the respective companies' estimated revenue figures for each day within the specified period. If data for all specified days is not released by 7:00 PM ET on the second day following the final date, this market will resolve based on data available at that time. Entries labeled “Other” will not be considered a model family for the purposes of this market. Revisions to published data will be considered only until all relevant data for the specified period has been released.

Model families will be ranked primarily by their total estimated inference revenue over the specified seven-day period, with alphabetical order of model family names as listed in this market group used as a tiebreaker (e.g., if two model families are exactly tied, “Anthropic” would be ranked ahead of “OpenAI”). This market will resolve based on the model family that occupies second place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is data published by Anera Intelligence at https://anera.markets/intelligence/model-family. If the relevant data for the full period is not available by 7:00 PM ET on the second day following the final date, this market will resolve based on data available at that time. If this resolution source is unavailable, this market will remain open until the data becomes available and will resolve based on the first subsequent check.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures (CL) have climbed to around $95.40 per barrel amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, including recent U.S. strikes on Iran-flagged tankers during fragile peace talks, embedding a supply disruption premium into current pricing. A larger-than-expected American Petroleum Institute (API) draw of 5.3 million barrels last week reinforced bullish sentiment, countering building global inventories projected by the EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook, which anticipates Brent peaking near $115/bbl in Q2 2026 before easing on non-OPEC+ production gains. Traders are positioning ahead of weekly EIA inventory releases—next on May 11—and potential OPEC+ output adjustments, with summer driving demand and macroeconomic risk appetite as key swing factors through June settlement.

markets.question: Will Olympique Lyonnais win on 2026-05-10?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 10, 2026
If Olympique Lyonnais wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Toulouse FC vs. Olympique Lyonnais
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Toulouse FC and Olympique Lyonnais.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olympique Lyonnais hold a slight edge in trader consensus at 52.5% implied probability for their Ligue 1 away win at Toulouse, driven by their third-place standing with 60 points and a four-match winning streak featuring 11 goals, including triumphs over PSG and Rennes, boosting Champions League qualification hopes. Toulouse, secure in 10th at 41 points with no relegation pressure, sit at 22.5% after a recent 2-1 away win over Strasbourg but only one victory in their prior five league games and multiple home struggles. Key absences plague the hosts—Mario Sauer suspended, Abu Francis, Frank Magri, and Rafik Messali injured—while Lyon boasts a near-full squad despite Tanner Tessmann's season-ending muscle issue. The reverse fixture's 2-1 Toulouse upset adds competitiveness, alongside viable 24.5% draw odds amid Lyon's modest away record.

Tianjin Jinmen Tiger, mired in 16th place with a 2-2-4 record marked by recent losses to Liaoning Tieren (3-0) and Shenzhen Pengcheng (1-0) but a morale-boosting 1-1 draw versus Dalian Yingbo on April 12, leverage strong home form and a 2-1 head-to-head victory over Chengdu in July 2025 to stay within striking distance at 45%, underscoring the matchup's even table dynamics and upset potential despite no major confirmed injuries or suspensions.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Melanie Stansbury's strong reelection bid in safely Democratic New Mexico's 1st Congressional District, rated D+7 by Cook Political Report, underpins trader consensus pricing Democrats at over 90% to retain the seat after her 13-point 2024 victory. The district's partisan lean, encompassing Albuquerque and surrounding areas, combined with Stansbury's uncontested Democratic primary on June 2 and superior fundraising, solidifies this commanding position amid no notable developments in the past 30 days. While Republicans hold slim odds, a shift could arise from a surprise GOP primary standout, personal scandal, health issues for Stansbury, or a national midterm wave favoring challengers before the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:

If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

The race remains tightly contested due to Lula's stagnant approval ratings near 40%, economic pressures, and his age of 80 raising voter concerns, while Flávio benefits from family brand loyalty without facing the same baggage. Separation could arise from gubernatorial endorsements like those from Tarcísio de Freitas or Romeu Zema, upcoming debates, or shifts in inflation and crime data ahead of the October 4 first round and potential runoff.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% for a third consecutive meeting on April 29-30, 2026, amid resurgent inflation with March CPI accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year from 2.4% prior, driven by energy costs, while April unemployment remained stable at 4.3% signaling labor market resilience. The March dot plot median projects a slight decline to 3.4% by end-2026, yet CME FedWatch futures imply near-certainty of no change at the June 16-17 FOMC, reflecting trader consensus for prolonged restrictive policy absent cooling price data. Key catalysts include tomorrow's April CPI release on May 12 and subsequent meetings through December, which could shift the path toward easing or hikes before 2027.

markets.question: Will Erling Haaland be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 English Premier League season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025-26 EPL season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No". 

Goals scored in Premier League matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., FA Cup, Carabao Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market. 

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Erling Haaland holds a commanding four-goal lead in the Premier League Golden Boot race with 26 goals to Brentford's Igor Thiago's 22, a gap widened by Haaland's recent strikes including a brace in Manchester City's latest win, as confirmed in official standings updated within the past day. With roughly four matches remaining before the May 24 finale, City face a favorable schedule against lower-table sides, bolstering trader consensus on Haaland's lock via his elite finishing (23.98 xG), high shot volume, and minimal injury concerns from recent reports. Thiago's breakout campaign keeps him as a distant second at 1% implied probability, but realistic challenges require Haaland injury, suspension, or total blank while Thiago nets multiples—scenarios deemed improbable given current form and fixture edges.

markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Anthropic at 94% implied probability for the best coding AI model by end of May, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's April 2026 release, which achieved leading scores like 87.6% on SWE-bench Verified and 77.8% on SWE-bench Pro—outpacing OpenAI's GPT-5.5 (88.7% max but trailing in agentic tasks) and Google's Gemini 3.1. Anthropic's Claude Code agent further bolsters this edge through superior context management and multi-step software engineering on benchmarks like LiveCodeBench. This reflects real capital backing sustained coding superiority amid tight competition. Challenges could arise from unannounced OpenAI or Google releases before May 31, new evaluations favoring rivals, or benchmark updates exposing gaps in real-world deployment.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's highly anticipated ninth studio album, Iceman—his first full-length solo project since 2023's For All the Dogs—drops May 15 via OVO Sound and Republic Records, driving intense speculation on lyrical content in this Polymarket. Traders focus on whether specific terms like artist names (Kendrick Lamar, Kanye), personal nods ("Daddy," reflecting fatherhood themes), or cultural references ("Covid," Toronto, LeBron/Bronny) appear in official Spotify or Apple Music transcripts. Hype stems from Toronto ice-block pop-ups unveiling the date, Adin Ross streams teasing insider details, and echoes of the 2024 Kendrick beef, alongside Drake's signature autobiographical bars blending industry jabs, hometown pride, and pop culture. With no confirmed tracklist leaks, sentiment hinges on historical patterns; resolution locks post-release, ahead of Billboard 200 debut battles.

markets.question: Will Ben Griffin win the 2026 Truist Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who wins the 2026 Truist Championship tournament.

If a listed player is eliminated from contention for the Truist Championship tournament based on the official rules of the tournament, the relevant market will immediately resolve to "No".

If an unlisted player wins the Truist Championship tournament this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by PGA Tour official tournament rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no winner is announced by May 16, 2026 at 8:00PM ET this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be the official results published by the PGA Tour website (https://www.pgatour.com/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Entering the final round of the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, Kristoffer Reitan commands trader consensus at 37.7% implied probability, one stroke behind leaderboard leader Alex Fitzpatrick (-14) after matching his third-round 64 with birdies on key par-4s and a stunning 84-foot Texas wedge eagle. Cameron Young's third-place position at -12, fueled by a scorching 63 Saturday including multiple long birdie putts, elevates him to 19.4%, while Sung-Jae Im holds T4 at -10 (19.2% market share) despite a third-round 70 fading his early 64-69 lead. The bunched leaderboard atop this $20M signature event reflects Reitan's surging momentum from Monday qualifier to contender, contrasting Fitzpatrick's steadier but pressure-tested path.

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Secret Whales secured LCP's second MSI 2026 slot on May 9 via championship points, marking the first qualification after their dominant Split 2 win over MVK Esports and capping a strong regular season run. Other regions head into decisive phases: LCK's top six from Rounds 1-2 gear up for the Road to MSI double-elimination Bo5 bracket starting June 6 in Wonju, where recent Week 6 results have Gen.G and T1 leading contention; LPL Split 2 sees Bilibili Gaming and JD Gaming clash in late matches amid tight standings; LEC Spring features Karmine Corp atop the table over G2 Esports and Natus Vincere, with playoffs looming by early June. LCS Spring, CBLOL Split 1, and LCP's first seed remain fluid, influenced by patch metas, roster health, and Bo5 intensity.

markets.question: Will Hylo launch a token by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Hylo officially launches a governance token by 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The token must be actively and publicly transferable and tradable. Announcements alone do not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Hylo (https://x.com/hylo_so), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Rizo Velovic win Survivor Season 50?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the contestant who wins Survivor season 50.

If Survivor season 50 concludes without a winner being declared, or if Survivor season 50 has otherwise not concluded by June 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET this market will resolve to "Other". In the case of a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the listed contestant whose name comes first in alphabetical order. 

This market will remain open until the conclusion of the season.

The resolution source for this market will be the official broadcast of the final episode of Survivor season 50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Aubry Bracco commands an overwhelming 87.5% implied probability as Survivor 50's frontrunner, propelled by her elevated strategic edit and jury appeal in the endgame, particularly after episode 11's soul-crushing blindside of Ozzy Lusth, which eliminated a physical threat and solidified her alliances among the final seven. Traders view her understated gameplay—navigating blindsides without overexposure—as a jury magnet, echoing past runner-up strengths from Kaôh Rōng and Game Changers, while Cirie Fields trails at 7% amid perceptions of her puppet-mastering backfiring in final Tribal scrutiny. Recent triple eliminations and immunity wins have pruned the field to Jonathan Young, Joe Hunter, and Rick Devens as distant spoilers, but with the May 20 finale looming, any immunity upset or bitter jury could spark volatility in this all-returnees milestone season.

markets.question: Will Benoni Mendes win the 2026 Minas Gerais gubernatorial election?
markets.description:
The Minas Gerais gubernatorial election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, with a runoff on October 25, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder governors appointed by any means before the specified election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Senator Cleitinho Azevedo (Republicanos) leads Polymarket odds at 56.5% implied probability for the October 4, 2026, Minas Gerais gubernatorial election, driven by consistent first-round polling leads in recent surveys, including Doxa (early May, 28%) and Genial/Quaest (late April, 30%), where he also prevails in most simulated second-round matchups against challengers. Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB), at 18.5%, benefits from his former Senate presidency and alliances with President Lula, though recent polls show him trailing at 6-21%. Fragmented opposition boosts Gabriel Azevedo (MDB, 12.1%) and ex-Belo Horizonte mayor Alexandre Kalil (PDT, 9.8%), with trader consensus anticipating Cleitinho's path through a crowded first-round field.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent House Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries' commanding position in the solidly Democratic NY-08 district, rated Solid D by Cook Political Report with a partisan lean exceeding D+25, drives trader consensus toward a 92% implied probability of Democratic victory on November 3, 2026. No credible Republican candidate has emerged for the general election, while Jeffries faces only a long-shot progressive primary challenge from NYC Councilmember Chi Ossé, filed in November 2025, ahead of the June 23 closed primary. Absent recent catalysts like scandals, high-profile GOP recruitment, or a national Republican wave flipping safe seats—scenarios with historical low base rates—the market reflects entrenched district demographics and incumbency advantages.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Morgan Stanley holds a commanding 42% implied probability as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated 2026 IPO due to its prominent role among the five active bookrunners—alongside Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, JPMorgan, and Citigroup—in the unprecedented 21-bank syndicate for Project Apex, valued at $1.75 trillion with a potential $75 billion raise. Recent reports from early April highlighted Morgan Stanley banker Michael Grimes' key involvement and E*Trade's talks to spearhead retail allocation, up to 30% of shares, bolstering its positioning amid SpaceX's Starlink profitability and Starship milestones. Goldman Sachs trails at 17.5% on similar tech IPO expertise, while others lag on lesser prominence. Traders await the S-1 filing by May 22 for clarity ahead of the June roadshow.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 35°C or higher on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (METMalaysia) latest forecast sets Kuala Lumpur's maximum temperature at 34°C for May 10, with isolated afternoon thunderstorms limiting solar heating through increased cloud cover and precipitation—driving trader consensus to near-certainty on this outcome via real-money positioning. This aligns with UK Met Office model runs projecting a 34°C peak amid sunny intervals and high humidity typical of the equatorial climate, where May highs at Kuala Lumpur International Airport station average 32–34°C based on historical data. Current morning observations match this cap, with feels-like temperatures near 36°C under moist conditions. A shift to prolonged clear skies could enable 35°C or higher via enhanced insolation, but forecast consensus deems it improbable; airport station readings will confirm resolution by day's end.

markets.question: Will Estonia be in the top 3 at Eurovision 2026?
markets.description:

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to finish as one of the top 3 highest scorers based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen with "Liekinheitin" dominate trader consensus for a Eurovision 2026 top 3 finish, trading around 45% implied probability to win outright on Polymarket following impressive second rehearsals that showcased dynamic staging and broad appeal. Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") and Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund follow closely at 12-13%, buoyed by strong pre-poll fan momentum and cultural hooks, though juries remain unpredictable per historical voting patterns. All 35 entries are set after national selections, with semis kicking off May 12 in Vienna—host city after Austria's 2025 victory—potentially shifting odds via qualification drama and televote previews ahead of the May 16 final.

markets.question: New York Mets vs. Arizona Diamondbacks: O/U 8.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the New York Mets and Arizona Diamondbacks, scheduled for May 10 at 4:10 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the New York Mets and Arizona Diamondbacks combine to score 9 or more runs in this game.

If the combined total is less than 9, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: New York Mets vs. Arizona Diamondbacks
events.description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the New York Mets and Arizona Diamondbacks, scheduled for May 10 at 4:10PM ET:

This market will resolve to "New York Mets" if the New York Mets win the game.

This market will resolve to "Arizona Diamondbacks" if the Arizona Diamondbacks win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Mets face the Arizona Diamondbacks in the series finale at Chase Field, where left-hander Eduardo Rodriguez (3-0, 2.50 ERA) starts for the hosts against Mets right-hander Huascar Brazobán (2-0, 1.53 ERA) as an opener, likely yielding bulk innings to struggling David Peterson (1-4, 6.29 ERA). Arizona evened the three-game set with a 2-1 win Saturday behind Merrill Kelly's seven dominant innings, limiting a Mets offense averaging just 3.62 runs per game. Both clubs languish below .500—Mets at 15-24 with a 6-4 mark in their last 10, Diamondbacks 18-20 but 2-8 lately—amid key absences like Mets' Kodai Senga (lumbar), Luis Robert Jr. (back), and Diamondbacks' Pavin Smith (elbow). Home-field edge and Rodriguez's form tilt trader consensus toward a tight contest as the Mets wrap a demanding West Coast road trip.

events.title: Club Blooming vs. Club Bolívar
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Bolivia LFPB game, scheduled for Sunday, May 10, 2026 between Club Blooming and Club Bolívar.

eventMetadata.context_description: Club Blooming holds a slim trader consensus edge as hosts against third-placed Bolívar in this tight División Profesional summit meeting, with both clubs unbeaten on 7 points from three matches—Blooming's 2-1-0 start featuring solid home form at Estadio Ramón Aguilera Costas. Bolívar's earlier 5-1 thrashing of Blooming in February at high-altitude La Paz underscores their head-to-head dominance (36 wins to Blooming's 7), but the switch to Santa Cruz's heat and lower elevation poses acclimatization challenges for the visitors, leveling the matchup. Recent clean sheets and attacking momentum from both keep probabilities bunched, highlighting the rivalry's unpredictability amid no major injury disruptions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects National Weather Service forecasts projecting a high near 83°F in Dallas on May 11 under partly sunny skies with east-northeast winds 5-10 mph, but tempered by likely afternoon showers and thunderstorms—some potentially severe—that could increase cloud cover and suppress peak heating. Model ensembles like GFS show spread in convective coverage; watch evening updates from NWS Fort Worth for refined guidance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Austria holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 43% implied probability for victory over Algeria (37.5%) in this pivotal 2026 FIFA World Cup Group J finale at neutral Arrowhead Stadium in Kansas City, with a draw at 29.5% underscoring the tight contest between evenly ranked sides (Austria 24th, Algeria 28th FIFA). Algeria's mounting injury crisis—goalkeepers Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, plus Yacine Titraoui (hamstring) and Amine Gouiri (muscle)—has eroded squad depth just weeks from kickoff, tempering their strong African qualifying campaign. Austria, buoyed by recent friendlies wins over Ghana (5-1) and South Korea (1-0) in late March, leverages superior recent form and defensive solidity despite their own concerns like Maximilian Wöber's muscular issues, keeping probabilities bunched in this high-stakes neutral-venue showdown absent recent head-to-head history.

markets.question: Will Liverpool finish in the top 4 of the EPL 2025–26 standings?

markets.question: Will Solana dip to $20 by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to “Yes” if any Binance 1-minute candle for Solana (SOL/USDT) between November 24, 2025, 14:30 and December 31, 2026, 23:59 in the ET timezone has a final “Low” price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT “Low” prices available at:

markets.question: Spread: FK Lokomotiv Moskva (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Russian Premier League game, scheduled for May 10 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FK Lokomotiv Moskva" if FK Lokomotiv Moskva win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FK Baltika Kaliningrad".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierliga.ru. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FK Lokomotiv Moskva vs. FK Baltika Kaliningrad - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Russian Premier League game, scheduled for May 10 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Lokomotiv Moscow, sitting third in the Russian Premier League table after 27 matches with 48 points, hosts fourth-placed Baltika Kaliningrad (46 points) on May 10 at RZD Arena in a pivotal late-season encounter for Champions League qualification spots. Lokomotiv's recent form shows inconsistency—losses to Krylya Sovetov (0-2) and Spartak Moscow (1-2), offset by a win over Orenburg (1-0)—while Baltika has drawn four straight before a 0-1 defeat to Akron Togliatti, boasting the league's stingiest defense (17 goals conceded). Head-to-head favors Lokomotiv with six wins in 11 meetings and no recent losses to Baltika; Lokomotiv enters at full strength, but Baltika misses winger Eldar Civic to injury. Home advantage and superior goal difference position Lokomotiv as trader consensus favorite amid tight standings.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.
